hypotheses.title: NFS1 Cys328 Redox Switch as Circadian Fe-S Assembly Gatekeeper
hypotheses.bridge: NFS1 Cys328 redox sensitivity + NAD+/NADH 30% circadian amplitude → 3-fold NFS1 activity oscillation
hypotheses.key_claim: NFS1 desulfurase activity oscillates ~3-fold over 24h via Cys328 redox modification, gating Fe-S assembly
hypotheses.key_risk: Mitochondrial matrix Trx2/Grx2 may buffer Cys328 against cytoplasmic redox oscillation

hypotheses.title: CISD2/NAF-1 as Circadian ER-Mito Calcium Timer via [2Fe-2S] Lability
hypotheses.bridge: CISD2 3Cys:1His pH/redox-labile [2Fe-2S] cluster at MAMs + triple circadian input convergence
hypotheses.key_claim: CISD2 cluster occupancy oscillates circadianly, modulating ER-mito Ca2+ transfer and creating an NADH feedback loop
hypotheses.key_risk: CISD2 cluster more stable in vivo than estimated; aging phenotype may mask circadian effects

hypotheses.title: Peroxiredoxin Cycle as Non-Transcriptional Fe-S Clock via H2O2-Mediated Cluster Destruction
hypotheses.bridge: Prx 24h H2O2 waves + Fe-S cluster oxidative destruction + transcription-independent oscillation
hypotheses.key_claim: Fe-S enzyme activity oscillates 24h even in BMAL1 KO or enucleated cells, mediated by Prx-generated H2O2
hypotheses.key_risk: H2O2 concentration during Prx hyperoxidation may be below Fe-S destruction threshold in vivo

hypotheses.title: IRP1 [4Fe-4S] Cluster Occupancy as Mechanistic Driver of Diurnal IRE-mRNA Control
hypotheses.bridge: IRP1 aconitase/IRE-BP switch + 3h cluster half-life + Nadimpalli 2024 gap (unmeasured occupancy)
hypotheses.key_claim: IRP1 Fe-S cluster occupancy (not protein level) oscillates ~2-fold over 24h, driving diurnal ferritin/TfR1 regulation
hypotheses.key_risk: IRP2 10-fold transcriptional oscillation may be the dominant driver over IRP1 cluster occupancy

hypotheses.title: Chronopharmacology of NFS1 Inhibitors: Compound 53 and D-Cysteine as Time-of-Day-Dependent Modulators
hypotheses.bridge: Compound 53 (IC50=16.3µM) + D-cysteine (Kdapp=25.6µM) + predicted NFS1 circadian oscillation
hypotheses.key_claim: Compound 53 cytotoxicity varies ≥2-fold between circadian peak and trough of NFS1 activity
hypotheses.key_risk: Depends on H1 (NFS1 oscillation); Compound 53 off-target effects may swamp circadian signal

hypotheses.title: GLRX5 as Circadian Fe-S Distribution Bottleneck via GSH/GSSG Oscillation
hypotheses.bridge: GLRX5 GSH-dependent [2Fe-2S] transfer + squared GSH effect + 4h phase delay prediction
hypotheses.key_claim: GLRX5 creates a distribution bottleneck with ~4h phase delay: cytoplasmic Fe-S proteins lag behind mitochondrial
hypotheses.key_risk: Alternative transfer pathways (HSC20, NFU1) may compensate; 55% tracking may be below detection

hypotheses.title: BMAL1-AMPK-PGC-1a-NFS1 Transcriptional Cascade as Mammalian Analog of Drosophila NFS1-Clock Phenotype
hypotheses.bridge: AMPK (sole KEGG circadian-metabolism bridge node) → PGC-1α → NFS1 transcription
hypotheses.key_claim: NFS1 is a specific rate-limiting PGC-1α target, creating a transcriptional arm complementary to Cys328 redox gating
hypotheses.key_risk: Vocabulary re-description risk: may reduce to generic clock → AMPK → mitochondrial gene coupling

hypotheses.title: Frataxin Iron Donation as Circadian Fe-S Rheostat Gated by Hepcidin-Driven Labile Iron Oscillation
hypotheses.bridge: Frataxin-ISCU2 iron donation + FDX2:FXN stoichiometry (Lill 2025 Nature) + hepcidin circadian LIP oscillation
hypotheses.key_claim: FA heterozygous carriers (50% frataxin, 1:100) show reduced circadian amplitude of Fe-S enzyme activities
hypotheses.key_risk: Ferritin buffering may dampen LIP oscillation; FA carrier phenotype may be too subtle to detect
